title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein explores how imperfections, like dents and burn marks, symbolize friendship, partnership, and personal growth.
Yowei Shaw shares a story of a burnt dining table as a lesson in personal and relationship growth.
Listeners are invited to share photos of meaningful imperfections that represent community, love, or learned lessons.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opens a cosmetology salon with 25 stations for student training in hair, skin, and nail care.
The salon allows students to gain real-world experience working with clients while accruing 1,000 training hours.
Students can work toward professional cosmetology licensure, with instructor-supervised services available to the public by appointment.

title: CeCarré brings a $48 gold-and-caviar-topped pizza to the Pru
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: CeCarré Pizza & Provisions opened at the Prudential Center on March 7, replacing the Detroit-style Descendant Pizza.
The menu features Roman-style pizzas, including a $48 pizza topped with 23-karat gold and caviar.
Most pizzas are priced around $26 and include unique toppings like kimchi, hot dogs, and french fries.

title: Mass. woman killed in 6-vehicle crash in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 69-year-old Massachusetts woman died in a six-vehicle crash on I-75 in Ochopee, Florida.
The crash was initiated by a semi-truck failing to slow, causing a chain-reaction collision.
The SUV driver and another Florida resident were seriously injured and hospitalized.

title: Former Miss Hall’s School teacher at center of grooming allegations is charged with rape
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matthew Rutledge, former teacher at Miss Hall's School, indicted on three rape charges involving students.
Rutledge accused of sexual misconduct and grooming during his tenure from 1991 to 2024, per DA's office.
Miss Hall's 2025 report linked Rutledge to multiple misconduct cases and alleged boundary crossings.

title: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in Boston for the World Cup, affecting accommodations for the event this summer.
Boston to host seven World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium, rebranded as Boston Stadium, from June 13 to July 9.
Meet Boston anticipates room availability changes, developing strategies to sell rooms to transient visitors and fans.

title: ‘I had just lost everything’: Kelsey Fitzsimmons testifies in her trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Fitzsimmons testified about being shot by Officer Patrick Noonan during a June 2025 incident involving a restraining order.
Closing arguments in the Essex County Superior Court bench trial are scheduled for Thursday morning.

title: In the midst of the MVP race, Jaylen Brown explains his stance on the NBA’s 65-game rule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown critiques the NBA's 65-game rule, suggesting 62 games might be more reasonable for awards eligibility.
Despite issues with Cade Cunningham missing MVP eligibility, Brown supports a game requirement to prevent awards for part-time participation.
Brown is on track for the 65-game mark, enhancing his position in the MVP race, while many contenders risk ineligibility.

title: ‘It felt like a gut punch’: Pride flags removed from BU offices ignite free speech, inclusion debate
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University removed Pride flags from offices citing a 'content-neutral' policy limiting outward-facing signage.
Faculty and students argue this enforcement undermines free speech and LGBTQ+ visibility, sparking campus-wide debate.
Legal concerns arise over the policy's consistency and potential violation of Massachusetts Civil Rights Act protections.

title: David Ortiz won Boston.com’s bracket of the best sports soundbites, and it wasn’t close
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: David Ortiz's 'This is our f****** city' won Boston.com's best sports soundbite bracket by a wide margin.
Ortiz's 2013 Fenway Park statement received the highest number of votes, highlighting Boston's sports spirit.
Kevin Millar's 'Don't let us win today' was the runner-up, surprising many with its unexpected bracket success.
